meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no design skills (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no design skills. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve No Design Skills
content.seo_description: Learn to implement professional user onboarding to hide design flaws. A high-impact guide for students using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: When design skills lag, focus on user onboarding to create a guided, high-value experience. By using Antigravity to scaffold structured layouts and Claude Code to automate interaction logic, you transform a plain interface into a professional, step-by-step product tour that keeps users engaged and focused on functionality.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks grinding on logic, database schemas, and API integrations, only to stare at a screen that looks like a prototype from 1998. The 'no design' anxiety is real, and it’s the silent killer of student developer motivation. You feel like your hard work is invisible because the surface layer lacks polish.

But here is the engineering secret: users don't care about your CSS skills; they care about their immediate success. By implementing a high-fidelity user onboarding flow, you turn a lack of design into a feature. You guide the user through the 'Aha!' moment, masking UI simplicity with intent-driven navigation and clear, actionable focus.

steps.title: Define the Value Path
steps.description: Identify the three critical interactions a user must take to see value. We will use this to structure the onboarding sequence.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a 3-step onboarding flow schema for a SaaS app. Define the state machine that transitions the user from landing to the primary dashboard feature.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a JSON schema for a step-by-step onboarding walkthrough state object'

steps.title: Build the Modal Scaffold
steps.description: Generate a clean, responsive modal component that serves as the container for your onboarding steps.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a reusable React modal component using Tailwind CSS that accepts 'step' props and handles transition animations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create OnboardingModal.tsx using Tailwind classes for a clean, professional look'

steps.title: Implement Contextual Highlighting
steps.description: Use overlays to dim the background, forcing the user to focus on specific UI elements during the tour.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a React hook 'useHighlight' that adds an overlay to the DOM, isolating a target DOM element for the onboarding tour.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a useHighlight hook that dims the page and highlights a specific element ID'

steps.title: Logic Injection with Claude
steps.description: Connect the onboarding state to your app's user profile so that the tour only runs once.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service function that tracks onboarding completion status in localStorage and syncs with a user settings API endpoint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement an onboarding persistence layer that checks if the user has completed the tour'

steps.title: Final Polish & Feedback Loop
steps.description: Ensure the onboarding feels snappy and responsive across mobile and desktop devices.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refine the onboarding component CSS to ensure it is fully responsive and accessible for keyboard-only navigation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'Run accessibility audit on the onboarding flow and fix any contrast issues'

pro_tips: Never onboard users to features they don't need; focus exclusively on the 'Aha!' moment.
Use 'progressive disclosure'—show only what the user needs to know at this exact second.
Keep copy brief; users will skip long paragraphs, but they will read short, punchy benefit statements.
Always provide an 'Exit Tour' button; forcing a user through a long flow is a primary cause of churn.
Use subtle motion (framer-motion) to make the onboarding feel like a native app experience rather than a series of popups.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding is live, track your conversion rate from 'New User' to 'First Action' using an analytics tool like PostHog to see if your tour is actually helping.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to turn a functional prototype into a polished, user-friendly SaaS. Don't let design roadblocks stop your momentum. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
